summary:
The first-line treatment for this 7-year-old男孩 with idiopathic nocturnal enuresis is behavioral intervention using an enuresis alarm, which has strong evidentiary support with up to 75% success rate and consistent endorsement across major pediatric guidelines. This approach promotes bladder awareness and nighttime awakening more effectively than reassurance alone and should be initiated before considering medication.  

Final Answer: \boxed{B}

Correct summary based strictly on provided text:

The agent concludes that the best initial treatment for childhood nocturnal enuresis is behaviorally driven enureisis alarm therapy due to robust evidence supporting its >75% success rate and alignment with worldwide pediatric guidelines. It explicitly rejects routine use of imaging, testing, or pharmacologic agents such as oxybutynin/imipramine unless indicated later, emphasizing instead early implementation of conditioning techniques aimed at improving bladder control during sleep.
